searchIndexBuilding: Preparing search on this device… (a few seconds; you can keep reading the map)
searchIndexFailed: Search could not finish preparing on this device. You can keep using the course—try closing search and opening it again later. If it keeps happening, try another browser or reload the page.
searchBookmarksEmpty: Start reading to save your place here.
tourConBody0: Tap a module to organize it: modules group topics, and lessons are the pages learners will read.
tourConBody2: When you want others to see changes, publish the course online (or update the public copy).
tourConBody3: Optional: AI helps plan or improve the curriculum.
tourConBody4: Versions and how to share the course (link, publish): see “More”.
tourConBody5: Return to the last saved version if needed.

uiTourSteps.title: The knowledge map
uiTourSteps.body: This is your forest: drag the background to pan, and tap or click the circles to open branches and lessons.

uiTourSteps.title: Back to the map
uiTourSteps.body: The logo returns you to the tree home when you want a clean overview.

uiTourSteps.title: Trees & libraries
uiTourSteps.body: Switch curriculum, load other knowledge trees, or manage content versions.

uiTourSteps.title: Search topics
uiTourSteps.body: Open search to jump to a topic by name without walking every branch.

uiTourSteps.title: Arcade
uiTourSteps.body: Play friendly practice games that can adapt to what you are studying.

uiTourSteps.title: Construction Mode
uiTourSteps.body: Turn this on to add folders and lessons, or reorganize editable trees.

uiTourSteps.title: Profile & backpack
uiTourSteps.body: Your avatar opens the menu: profile, export progress, achievements, and more.

uiTourSteps.title: Online progress sync (optional)
uiTourSteps.body: If you enable it, your progress can sync across devices (encrypted). Keep it off if you prefer local-only.

uiTourSteps.title: Full handbook
uiTourSteps.body: The detailed guide lives here: navigation, garden, AI, data, and shortcuts.

tourSourcesPickerBody: Search or pick a curriculum here. When you study, tap the owl in the dock for questions, summaries, and optional AI.

uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.title: Sage and choosing a tree
uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.body: Search or pick a curriculum here. When you study, tap the owl in the dock for questions, summaries, and optional AI.

uiTourStepsMobile.title: The map
uiTourStepsMobile.body: Explore by dragging and tapping the circles on the map to open topics and lessons.

uiTourStepsMobile.title: Home
uiTourStepsMobile.body: Return to the main tree view from anywhere.

uiTourStepsMobile.title: Search
uiTourStepsMobile.body: Find lessons by name without crossing the whole map.

uiTourStepsMobile.title: Arcade
uiTourStepsMobile.body: Practice games tied to what you are learning.

uiTourStepsMobile.title: More
uiTourStepsMobile.body: Handbook, trees, achievements, language, and tools live here. Profile and theme sit at the top right.

welcomeCloudSyncBodyShort: When on, progress can follow you to another computer or phone on the same browser profile; data is encrypted in transit. When off, everything stays only on this device.
welcomeCloudSyncOffRisk: With sync off, if you clear browser data or change devices without exporting a backup from Profile, you may lose local progress.
welcomeEnterGardenLicenseReminder: All shared trees are free to reuse (CC BY-SA 4.0).
welcomeEnterGardenTermsLink: All shared trees are free to reuse (CC BY-SA 4.0).
treesCcLicenseShort: All shared trees are free to reuse (CC BY-SA 4.0).
aiPitchSub: Tap the owl 🦉 whenever you are ready.
moreMenuRowForumSub: Topics and posts by course area
forumStructureHint: The list follows the course tree: each step to the right sits inside the section above. The tree publisher sets forum moderation. Use the search field to filter sections by name.
forumNewTopicOpened: Topic published with your first message.
forumEmptyNewTopicTitle: No posts in this topic yet
forumEmptyNewTopicBody: This topic has no messages yet. Write the first one in the box below.
forumThreadNoMessagesHint: First post: use the box at the bottom, then Publish post.
forumNewTopicNeedsBody: Add the first message too (title alone is not enough).
forumNoTree: To use the forum, open a course from Trees first.
forumPostFailed: Could not send your message. Check your connection and try again.
forumLoginRequired: You must log in to participate in the forum.
forumLoginRequiredBody: You must be logged in to write in the forum.
forumThreadClipped: Showing the most recent {n} posts in this topic. Older posts are hidden to keep the forum fast.
forumDailyLimit: Daily posting limit reached. Try again tomorrow.
passkeyUnavailable: Passkeys are not available in this environment.
passkeyNoAccountBody: No passkey is registered for “{username}” yet. Create one now?
passkeyLocalOnlyBody: Your progress stays local until you connect a passkey account.
profileLogoutClearsLocalButton: Sign out and delete this device
profileLogoutClearsLocalConfirm: This signs you out and deletes Arborito local data from this browser.
passkeyConnectBeforeSync: Connect a passkey account before enabling sync.
syncLoginConnectBeforeSync: Sign in with QR, code, or secret file (above) before enabling sync.
syncLoginLocalBody: Without sign-in, progress stays on this device. Sign in below to use it on more devices.
syncLoginRegisterLead: You get QR, text, and download — same secret.
syncLoginThreeWaysLead: Sign in with any of these (same account):
syncLoginCreateHint: Choose a username above. You will get a QR, a readable code, and a file — use whichever you like on each device.
syncLoginSecretNotInSession: This session does not have your secret (for example after a full reload). Sign in again with your code or file, or generate a new secret below.
profileImportReplaceBody: This replaces your current progress and profile on this device with the imported file. This cannot be undone. Continue?
profileLocalOnlyFooterLead: You are not signed in online. You can still erase everything Arborito stores in this browser if you want a clean start (you will be asked to confirm).
syncLoginQrUnreadable: Could not read a sync QR from that image.
syncLoginQrSignalHint: Scan this QR with your mobile to authorize sign-in.
syncLoginQrAuthRequired: Sign in with your sync code first to authorize other devices.
syncLoginQrSignalExpired: QR code expired. Generate a new one.
syncLoginQrExpired: QR code expired. Please try again.
syncLoginQrSignalInstructions: Open Arborito on your mobile device, go to Profile, and scan this QR code to sign in.
syncLoginQrSignalHint1: Make sure you are signed in on your mobile device.
syncLoginQrSignalHint2: This QR code is for one-time use only.
syncLoginFileUnreadable: That file is not a valid secret export.
syncLoginUsernameTaken: That username is already taken. Pick another or sign in.
syncLoginNoAccount: No account found for that name.
syncLoginCreatedOk: Account ready. Save QR, code, or file — your choice.
syncLoginRotateOnlySync: Only sync-code accounts can rotate the secret here.
syncLoginRotatedOk: New secret generated. Old QR, code, and file no longer work.
syncLoginProfileUsesNameHint: The name at the top is your online username when you register.
syncLoginProfileAuthedLead: If you lost backups, generate a new secret (other devices must sign in again).
syncLoginRenameConfirmBody: Only the name you type to sign in changes; the secret stays the same. Other devices use the new name when signing in.
syncLoginRotateWarnBody: Old QR, text, and backup file stop working. Other devices must sign in again with the new set.
syncLoginDeleteConfirm: Removes network sign-in for this name. Progress on this device stays; other devices stop syncing with this account.
syncLoginLegacyPasskeyNote: This account uses a passkey. Backup codes and recovery kit still apply below.
forumNeedSyncLogin: Open Profile and sign in with QR, code, or secret file to use the forum.
forumParticipationNeedLoginTitle: Sign in to use the forum
forumParticipationNeedLoginBody: Public courses use the same online account as publishing. Open Profile and sign in with a sync code or passkey (Register / Sign in under Online account).
identityQrInvalid: This QR is not a valid Arborito identity card.
recoverySectionLead: While signed in, save backup codes or an encrypted file so you can recover on another device if you lose this one.
recoveryHowOpenAssistant: How do I recover my account?
recoveryCodesReplaceWarning: This replaces any previous codes on the server. Save the new list in a safe place.
recoveryExportPassphraseBody: Choose a strong passphrase. It is not sent to any server; you will need it to open this file later.
recoveryAssistantLead: Enter your username and either a one-time backup code or your recovery file plus its passphrase. Then you can register a new passkey on this device.
recoveryBackupCodeHint: Use one of the codes you saved from your profile. Each code works once.
recoveryCodeOk: Code accepted. You can register a new passkey now.
recoveryCodeInvalid: That code is not valid or was already used.
recoveryKitFileHint: Pick the JSON file you exported from your profile and enter the same passphrase you used when creating it.
recoveryKitUnlocked: File unlocked. You can register a new passkey now.
recoveryRegisterPasskeyCta: Register new passkey on this device
recoveryNeedPasskey: Sign in with your passkey first.
recoveryBootstrapMissing: Unlock recovery with a code or file first.
recoveryEntryQrHelp: This QR does not log anyone in. It only opens the recovery screen in Arborito so you or someone helping you can use a code, file, or passkey.
profileAdvancedSummary: QR & contacts (not required to use the app)
profileContactsCardLead: Show your QR so someone can save you, or scan/paste theirs. This is not sign-in and does not share your account.
identityCardLeadAdvanced: Show your QR so someone can save you, or scan/paste theirs. Not a substitute for signing in.
forumLimitNotice: Privacy note: this forum is community-hosted. Arborito hides posts older than 30 days on this device. In P2P networks, we cannot guarantee deletion of copies stored by third parties.
forumMobileInThreadZoneHint: Use Back (←) to return to the topic list and change section.
sourcesDismissNeedTree: Add or load a tree before closing.
sourcesGlobalEmptyLead: Paste a link below, use quick add, or open My Garden to plant a local tree.
sourcesLocalGardenBannerTitle: You are currently tending your local garden.
sourcesLocalGardenBannerHint: Load a tree from the list below to return to the global forest.
sourcesOneLinkHint: Type library, an 8-character share code (e.g. A3F7-K2M9), or paste the full link someone sent you—the app figures it out. Official short names are resolved on this device only.
sourcesAliasPrivacyNote: Short names are looked up from a built-in list in the app (GDPR-friendly: no extra tracking).
sourcesUnknownAlias: Unknown name “{name}”. Try “library”, an 8-character code, or paste a full link.
sourcesAddDuplicate: That tree is already in your list.{hint}
forumNoThreadsInPlace: No topics in this section yet.
forumEmptyThreadsSubtitle: You can also use “+ New topic” in the header.
forumMasterSelectSectionHint: Pick a section on the left to see discussions for that area.
forumMasterSelectTopicHint: Then choose a topic in the middle list to read messages and join in.
forumPickThread: Choose a topic from the list
forumPickThreadLead: Open a topic from the list to read and post below. «+ New topic» asks for a title and the first message together.
forumPickThreadBody: Select a topic in the list. The writing box appears after you open one.
forumReplyDisabled: Choose a topic from the list first…
forumNewTopicSheetHint: Add a title and the opening post for «{place}». Others can reply to the topic or nest replies under any message.
forumNewTopicPlaceholder: Short title, e.g. Question about lesson 3
forumPostShortcutHint: Optional: Ctrl+Enter (⌘+Enter on Mac) to send faster
forumAccountDangerHint: Only if you need it: remove your presence for this course on the network. This does not delete files on this device.
forumDeleteMyAccountConfirmFinal: Final confirmation: remove your online identity for this course? You can still use the course here; only the network account is cleared.
publicTreeConfirmBody:
You are about to publish a full copy of this tree: lessons, forum messages, and progress data bundled with the package (what learners have saved locally and what you ship in this bundle).

You can retract later from this device if you keep the publisher key (Construction → Retract). Honest peers will stop showing the tree; malicious mirrors might still retain data.

Never include passwords, personal data, or anything confidential.
publicTreeSuccessLead: Share the code and short link below so others can open the same course in Arborito.
publicTreeSuccessNote: Anyone with the link can read the tree, forum, and the progress snapshots in this bundle. Use Retract in construction mode to signal the network to drop this tree (this device must keep the publisher key). Malicious peers might still retain data — never publish secrets.
publicTreeSuccessTorrentIndexLag: If you use the optional torrent browse mirror, it refreshes in batches: your tree can take a few minutes to show up there. Share code and links work right away; Nostr search is usually faster.
publicTreeSuccessCodeHint: Anyone can type this code under Trees → Add a tree (letters and numbers only, no account).
publicTreeCodeAllocFailed: Could not allocate a share code. Try again in a moment.
publicTreePublishTimeout: Publishing took too long (network or peers may be slow). If a loading state is stuck, reload the page, check your connection, and try again.
publicTreePublishWtTimeout: Optional WebTorrent packaging took too long; publishing continues without that layer.
publicTreePublishNoResult: Publishing did not finish (no public link). Check relays or try again.
publicTreePublishNoResultHint: Often this means no Nostr relay accepted the upload: check your network and that the browser allows secure WebSockets (wss://). If you maintain this Arborito deployment, see docs/NOSTR_RELAYS_CONFIGURATION.md in the repository.
publicTreePublishFailedBody:
Something went wrong while publishing online.

Technical detail: {detail}
publicTreePublishEventPackageBody:
The app could not build a valid message for the network. This is usually about the course data or size, not your Wi‑Fi or relays.

You can try:
• Shortening or splitting very large lessons
• Removing odd characters from pasted text (invisible symbols, broken emojis)
• Exporting a backup (.arborito), then updating Arborito and trying again

Technical note (for support): {detail}
publicTreePublishRelayBody:
Publishing needs at least one working WebSocket relay (wss://). Check your connection or try another network. If you maintain this Arborito deployment, see docs/NOSTR_RELAYS_CONFIGURATION.md in the repository.

Technical detail: {detail}
publishConfirmUnexpected: The publish dialog closed without a clear confirmation. Try again; if the problem persists, reload the page.
publicTreeDockTooltip: Publish a copy online to share it
publicTreeRepublishBody: This updates the public copy of this course on the network (same links and share code). Learner progress in the bundle is still minimized. Continue?
publicTreeRepublishSuccessLead: Your public course was updated. Existing links still work.
publicTreeRepublishDockTooltip: Review and publish changes to the public copy
publicTreeUpdateTooltip: Review and publish changes to the public copy
publicTreeUpToDateTooltip: Public copy is up to date
publishDiffNoChanges: No changes since the last publish.
publishDiffNoBaseline: No published snapshot was found for this local tree yet. Publish once to establish a baseline.
exportReleaseSnapshotsBody:
Choose which saved versions go inside the file. Anything you leave unchecked is omitted (smaller download).

Close with ✕ if you do not want to export right now.
exportArchiveGuideTxt:
Arborito export (.arborito file)

This file is JSON (pretty-printed). Open it in any text editor.

Structure:
- magic / version: archive format id.
- meta: export time and garden name.
- tree: curriculum (map + lessons); lesson bodies are Markdown in each leaf node's "content". Saved versions appear under `tree.releaseSnapshots` only if you checked them when exporting.
- files: INTRO.md and README.md mirror the course intro; EXPORT-GUIDE.txt is this note.

What this file does not include: forum conversations (they remain in the browser or on the network for public trees). It also does not include the browser search index — Arborito rebuilds search from the curriculum after import.

Nothing here is encrypted unless you add your own tools. Re-import: Arborito → Trees → Import (.arborito).
treePresentationReaderHintEmbed: The author has not added public information for this tree. The full introduction is below in Tree information, or report a problem with this tree.
releasesModalDesc: Snapshots and live for this curriculum.
treeLiveSeedsHint: For public trees on the network, this is a rough “seed” count: browsers that were recently active with the course open. More seeds usually means a livelier shared tree (it is not an exact headcount of people).
treeLiveSeedsLine: {n} seeds on the network now (approx.) — tree vitality
treeLiveSeedsLocalLine: Local tree: network “seeds” do not apply here (this device only).
treePresMobileCompactHint: Public summary, author & donation link — tap to edit
treeMetaDescriptionPh: Short summary for learners (map and full intro modal)
treeMetaDonationStripeHint: Only Stripe payment URLs are accepted: buy.stripe.com or donate.stripe.com. Anything else is ignored for safety.
treeDonationStripeInvalid: That URL is not a valid Stripe payment link. Use buy.stripe.com or donate.stripe.com.
treePresentationReaderHint: The author has not added information for this tree. Open the full introduction, or report a problem with this tree to Treesys.
treeReportPolicyBody:
Your report is stored safely (reason and who sent it) so the app can weigh it. It does not delete lessons or remove the course from the internet.

If many people report the same course in a short time, it may stop appearing in the public list inside the app. The app does that on its own—no one at Treesys reads every report like a support chat.

Copyright issues use a separate legal process described in the app’s legal notices.

Normal reports do not email the distributor. For the operator, use only the options below.
treeReportNeedPublishHint: Automatic reports apply to online courses (shared with others). If yours lives only on this device, publish it first or use the email section below.
treeReportSheetHint: Pick a reason. Sends are limited to prevent abuse.
treeReportAutoProcessNote: Sending counts toward the in-app listing rules; Treesys does not send a private reply for this button.
treeReportUrgentHint: Fill this only for urgent legal/security issues. This will prepare an email draft.
treeReportUrgentBodyPh: What happened? Include links, dates, and why it is urgent.
treeReportUrgentOpenEmailHint: This opens your email app with a prefilled draft.
treeReportUrgentToOwnerTitle: Urgent message to the tree owner
treeReportUrgentToOwnerLead: Use this before contacting the app operator. The course author may see a notice when they open it on their device. The Arborito distributor is not the recipient.
treeReportUrgentToOwnerMessagePh: Explain briefly and factually. This is not a private chat with the author.
treeReportUrgentToOwnerContactLabel: How the owner can reach you (optional)
treeReportUrgentToOwnerDisclaimer: By sending, you understand this goes to the course author, not to {operatorEmail}. Responding is up to whoever published the map, not the Arborito distributor.
treeReportUrgentToOwnerConfirmBody: This sends a notice to the course author. It does not email the app operator. Continue?
treeReportUrgentToOwnerSent: Urgent message stored for the tree owner. If that is not enough, use “Very urgent — operator” below.
treeReportUrgentSendFailed: Could not send the urgent message. Check your connection and try again.
treeReportUrgentToOwnerTooShort: Please write at least 40 characters in the message.
treeReportOperatorEscalationLead: Only if the owner route is insufficient—for example no response and serious harm. This opens an email draft to the distributor ({operatorEmail} by default), not to the tree owner.
treeReportOperatorEscalationBodyPh: Why the owner path was not enough, dates, links, and what you need from the operator.
treeReportOperatorEscalationMailHint: The draft is addressed to the operator inbox only.
creatorUrgentUserMessageToast: Someone left an urgent message for you on this course (not the app operator). Check “About this tree” if you publish contact there.
treeReportEmergencyHint: Only use this for urgent legal/security issues. Copy the email manually.
treeReportHint: Hiding a course from the in-app list does not delete copies someone saved or links outside Arborito.
treeReportEmailBodyHint: Describe the problem (e.g. illegal content, impersonation). Include screenshots if useful.
publishNeedLoginBody: To publish a tree to the network, sign in first—using a passkey or a sync code (Register / Sign in under Online account in Profile). After you tap OK here, Profile opens so you can do that. On mobile, Profile is often under More; on desktop, use your account entry.
publishMissingAuthor: Before publishing publicly, fill in Author name in “About this tree” on the map (construction mode). At least {n} characters.
publishMissingDescription: Before publishing publicly, add a short public description in “About this tree” on the map. At least {n} characters.
treeMetaMinCharsHint: Minimum {n} characters (required to save when changing these fields).
addCurriculumLangBody: Choose a preset language. The folder structure is copied from the language you are editing now; translate titles and lessons afterward.
pickCurriculumLangSubtitle: Tap a flag to add that language. Structure is copied from the language you are editing now.
pickCurriculumLangAllPresent: All preset languages are already in this tree.
addCurriculumLangInvalid: Choose a language from the list.
addCurriculumLangExists: That language is already in this tree.
addCurriculumLangDone: Language added. Switch languages with the dropdown and translate content in the graph.
addCurriculumLangNoTree: This tree has no language data to copy from.
addCurriculumLangProposerBlocked: Suggest-only role can’t add languages. Ask the tree owner for Editor access.
lessonTocAddSectionHint: Adds a large outline step in the lesson menu (below the part you are on).
lessonTocAddSubsectionHint: Adds a smaller step inside that part (indented in the lesson menu).
lessonTocDragNestHint: Drag to the right to nest deeper
lessonTocDropGutterNest: Drag to the right to nest deeper
lessonTocRemoveBlocked: At least one section must remain.
lessonTocEditHint: Edit and tap outside to save
lessonTocConstructToolbarAria: Add or remove lesson outline parts
lessonHeaderEditMeta: Edit lesson name, icon, and description
treeReadOnlyHint: This course is read-only. Open My garden (local tree) or your own course published online to edit.
nostrStructureEditHint: Structure edits stay in this tab’s memory until you publish the course to the public network.
authorLicenseSubtitle: Please read and accept before editing or publishing curriculum.
authorLicenseSubtitleReference: Reference text. Acceptance is recorded when you enter the garden or use authoring features.
authorLicenseReferenceNote: This panel is informational. You do not need to tap “accept” here—using My garden, construction mode, or publish applies the terms described.
authorLicenseSectionCC: By using authoring features (construction mode, publishing a course online, or creating a local garden), you agree that original educational content you create and share through Arborito is offered under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International license (CC BY-SA 4.0), unless you clearly state a different compatible license inside the material. You keep your copyright; CC BY-SA lets others share and adapt your work with credit and under the same terms.
authorLicenseSectionYou: You alone are responsible for the legality and suitability of what you publish. Do not upload unlawful material, malware, personal data you are not allowed to share, or content you do not have rights to distribute.
authorLicenseSectionArborito: Arborito is free / open-source software provided “as is”, without warranty of any kind. The Arborito and Treesys developers provide tools only: they do not host your trees, do not review lesson text for you, and are not the publisher of your content. They are not liable for user-created materials. For app issues, use About / Legal in the app; for illegal content on a network, contact the relevant host or authority.
authorLicenseCheckbox: I have read and accept the terms above, including CC BY-SA 4.0 for my original shared curriculum.
authorLicenseAcceptHint: Tapping Accept and continue means you agree to the terms above, including CC BY-SA 4.0 for curriculum you share.
noTreesLead: Add a public or private tree, or plant a new local garden to start exploring.
governanceForumModerate: You can clean up forum posts from Construction on this same browser.
roadmapExternalHint: Roadmap updates ship with app releases.
governanceNostrIntro: Link below. If you published here, you can invite editors.
governanceLocalHint: You are editing a course saved only in this browser.
governanceLocalNoActionsBody: Publish it to get a share link and invitations.
governanceModalIntro: The course link opens or adds the course on another device. Your code is your network identity—the publisher uses it to grant you edit access. To invite someone, paste their code in the section below.
governanceModalIntroOwner: Share the course code (above) so others can open the same tree under Trees → Add. To grant editing, paste the collaborator’s personal code in the section below.
governanceReaderNoRoleIntro: This course is administered by its author on the network. If you added it yourself (code or directory), you have read-only access until the author invites you. Send your personal code (below) to the author and wait for an Editor invitation.
governanceOwnerInviteCodeBlurb: Anyone who enters this code under Trees → Add opens the same course. Editing is granted when the author pastes the collaborator’s personal code below.
governanceOwnerInviteCodeMissing: No short code in metadata (older tree or not republished). Use the technical link below if needed.
governanceCourseLinkBlurb: This technical link opens or adds the course on another device; it does not grant editing by itself.
governanceCopyOpenLinkBtn: Copy link to open or add course
governanceNostrForumHint: Unpublish or moderate forum: same browser where you published (Construction).
governancePublicLinkNote: Unpublish or moderate forum: same browser where you published (Construction).
governanceLinkFootShort: Unpublish or moderate forum: same browser where you published (Construction).
governanceNoPublicTree: Open an online course to see the link.
governanceForumPolicyNeedNetwork: Open your published public tree to change forum settings.
governanceCollabIntro: Paste the person’s code. To be invited, send your code to the course admin.
governanceCollabOwnerOnly: Only whoever published from here can invite or remove.
governanceYourRoleEditor: Invited editor: you can edit in Construction. Publish or unpublish: owner only.
governanceYourRoleProposer: You have suggest-only access. Ask the owner for Editor to change lessons.
governanceYourPubHint: Send it to the course admin so they can invite you.
conSaveSuccess: Changes are in this session’s memory—publish to the network when you want others to see them.
conRevertInfo: Use the Trees list to switch to another course or reload this one from disk.
nostrUniverseRevokedError: This public tree was retracted by the publisher.
nostrLoadFailedError: Could not load this public tree.
curriculumLoadInvalidTreeJson: The course data is invalid or empty.
nostrBundleFormatUnsupported: This public course uses an old or incompatible package format. Ask whoever published it to republish with the current Arborito. Meanwhile you can ask for a .arborito file or another link if they have one.
nostrLessonLoadEmpty: (This lesson had no text on the network.)
nostrLessonLoadError: Could not load this lesson from peers. Try again or check your connection.
nostrIdentityUnavailable: For forum, publishing, or share codes with full encryption, open this page with https:// or from http://localhost. On a plain local address like http://192.168… the browser limits crypto: some online features may be unavailable or reduced.
nostrNotLoadedHint: Could not enable the connection for public online courses. Try reloading the page, another network, or opening Arborito with https://. Without it, share codes, publishing, and the online forum may be unavailable.
fetchSslTlsHint: That usually means the browser could not complete HTTPS with that server (old TLS, bad certificate, or the network is blocking that host). Try another Wi‑Fi or mobile data, another mirror URL if the tree offers one, or fix TLS on the server. Arborito cannot bypass your phone’s SSL checks.
revokePublicTreeDockTooltip: Retract this tree from the public network (requires publisher key on this device)
revokePublicTreeNoUniverse: Open your own public online course first.
revokePublicTreeNoKeyBody: Retracting requires the same browser profile that published this tree. If you cleared storage or use another device, you cannot sign a retraction from here.
revokePublicTreeConfirmBody: This will publish a retraction on the network and clear the bundle for cooperating peers. People who already copied data elsewhere might still have it. Continue?
revokePublicTreeSuccessBody: The public network was asked to stop serving this tree. You can publish again with a new link anytime.
forumModModeBanner: Moderation on (construction + publisher key): you can remove topics and messages.
forumModDeleteMessageConfirm: Remove this message? It won’t appear in new exports. Old shared links may still show it until you publish the tree again.
forumModDeleteThreadConfirm: Delete this topic and all messages? Old shared links may still show a copy until you publish the tree again.
forumModRepublishHint: Republish the public tree (construction dock) so new links replace the old snapshot.
constructionRequiresWritable: Construction mode only works on a course you can edit. Open one from My Garden or your own online course (this source is read-only).
forkNetworkTreeConfirmTitle: Create a local copy to edit?
forkNetworkTreeConfirmBody: This online tree is from another author (or you only have read-only access). We can create your own editable copy in My garden. Continue?
forkNetworkTreePromptBody: What should we call this tree in My garden?
forkNetworkTreeEmptyName: Please enter a name for the copy.
forkNetworkTreeInvalidData: This tree has no copyable curriculum loaded.
graphFolderToolsScopeHintRoot: New items are added at the first level of the course (under this start). Open a folder if you want to create something inside it.
mediaConsentBody: Only resources from the origins you approve below will load. Your browser will connect to those domains to fetch the image, audio, or video.
mediaConsentRememberHint: Choosing Load media stores only those domain origins in this browser (not the full URLs) so we will not ask again for them. Do not load keeps external media blocked here.
mediaConsentDontAskAgain: Remember these domains in this browser
mediaBlockedHint: This resource has not been loaded yet.
aboutTreesysProductLine: Arborito is a product of Treesys
missionText: Arborito is the open education platform driven by Treesys, the organization behind this ecosystem. Learn at your own pace, explore a living map of ideas, and give something back: a fix, a translation, a new lesson. Education is a human right, not a business—and every honest contribution helps the forest keep growing.
metaphorText: Like a tree, knowledge is nourished by the community (roots) to expand its branches and offer leaves of wisdom (lessons) to anyone who wishes to explore them.
impressumText:
Arborito™ is a product of Treesys®.

Liability Disclaimer: The software is provided 'as is', without warranty of any kind, express or implied. Treesys is not an accredited educational institution and does not confer academic degrees. Certificates generated by this software are for gamification and self-tracking purposes only.

External Links: We accept no liability for the content of external links (e.g., third-party knowledge trees). The operators of the linked pages are solely responsible for their content.

certBody: This certifies that the student has successfully completed the module:
sourceManagerDesc: Explore the global forest or plant your own garden.
sourceSwitchSuccess: Switched to a new knowledge universe
secWarningBody: You are about to save a link to a course we do not vet. Anyone can publish on the web—make sure the person or organization giving you the link is one you trust.
secWarningCheck: Check that the link origin matches who they claim to be (official website, known email, etc.):
secConfirm: Yes, I trust it—save this link
secLoadWarningBody: This link opens content we cannot verify for you. If you are unsure, cancel and ask the author for the link over a channel you already trust.
nostrPublicWarningBody: This link opens a course hosted on a shared public network (anyone with the link can join the same copy). Anything you write in the forum can be read by anyone with the link. Cloud progress sync is optional: only if you turn it on, encrypted with your key; you can turn it off in Profile. Do not post secrets or personal data you would not want shared.
nostrPublicWarningCheck: You can delete posts, remove your online identity for this course from the Forum, or talk to the course admin if something disappeared.
nostrAccountRemovedByAdminTitle: Your online account was removed from this tree
nostrAccountRemovedByAdminBody: You can still use the course here with your local progress in this browser. Your forum messages and synced progress for this tree were cleared from the public network. If you did not expect this, contact the course administrator to ask why.
forumSelfAccountRemovedBody: Your local learning progress in this browser is unchanged. Your forum posts and encrypted sync for this tree were removed from the network. If you post again, you will use a new identity.
forumNoPublicUniverse: This course has no online forum (it is not a shared public copy).
forumModUserRemovedOk: That account was removed from this tree online.
forumDeleteMyAccountConfirm: This removes your forum posts and synced progress for this tree from the public network. Local progress in this browser stays. Continue?
forumModBanUserConfirm: Ban this user from posting in this forum?
forumBannedCannotPost: You cannot post in this forum.
forumModUnbanUserConfirm: Unban this user so they can post again?
forumModRemoveUserConfirm: Remove this participant’s forum identity and encrypted progress for this tree? They can still open the course; only the online account is cleared.
errorNoContent: No valid content found in this tree.
fileNotFound: File not found in local garden.
saveFailedLocal: Local save failed. Node not found.
readmeIntroTreeCodeHint: Share code or public tree link. Paste it under Trees → Add to open this tree elsewhere.
exportDesc: Choose what you want to download.
emptyModuleDesc: This branch has no lessons yet. Open Trees & libraries to load another course, or add lessons in construction mode if you edit this tree.
emptyModuleConstructHint: In construction, this point is an empty folder: create a lesson so people have something to open.
pdfDisclaimerText:
This document was automatically generated by Arborito, a decentralized knowledge explorer.
Source Repository: {url}

The content, text, images, and intellectual property contained herein belong exclusively to the authors and maintainers of the external repository linked above. Arborito (Treesys) acts solely as a visualization engine and assumes no responsibility for the accuracy, legality, copyright status, or safety of the material presented in this document. Users are responsible for verifying the source of the information.
editorBlockMediaUrl: Link to video, audio, or image
editorBlockMediaUrlHint: Paste an address starting with https://…
editorBlockCallout: Write a tip or important note here…
editorBlockGameHint: This inserts an optional game item. Students will open the standard Game Player with this cartridge preselected.
editorBlockGameTopicsHint: These topics will be sent to the cartridge as context (topics=...).
sageHello: 🦉 Hoot hoot! I am awake. Ask me anything.
sageConfigDesc: Choose how the owl answers (optional AI).
sagePromptSummarize: Summarize the lesson "{lesson}" in 3 bullet points.
sagePromptExplain: Explain the main concept of the lesson "{lesson}" in simple terms.
sagePromptQuiz: Give me a test question about the lesson "{lesson}".
sageGdprText: This feature downloads a large AI model from the Hugging Face CDN to run locally in your browser.
sageGdprConnectsNote: This step uses third-party hosts (e.g. jsDelivr, Hugging Face) to download about 200MB+ of model data and your internet connection. Afterwards, the model runs on your device.
sageLoadingBrainDesc: Downloading the AI model to your browser. This usually happens only once.
sageMenuChatDesc: Ask the owl helper about this lesson.
sageContextPresetHint: Controls how much history and lesson evidence is sent to the model.
sageMaxNewTokensHint: Lower = faster and shorter answers; higher = longer replies.
sageBrowserModelHint: Uses wllama with Llama 3 GGUF models (quantized). Runs entirely in your browser using CPU/WebAssembly.
sageBrowserModelPublicHint: Public GGUF models from Hugging Face work without authentication. Only Llama 3 models are supported.
aboutPrivacyTabBlurb: By default your learning data stays in this browser. The sections below explain how GDPR applies to the app, public online trees (nostr://), forum and optional network sync, and what you can do.
privacyLocalFirstCallout: Local-first: Your learning progress is stored in this browser (LocalStorage). By default it is not sent to a central Treesys database.
privacyAiOllamaLine: Local AI (Ollama): Runs on your machine; traffic stays on your network or localhost.
privacyAiBrowserLine: In-browser AI (wllama): Only after you accept the download prompt in Sage. The runtime loads from jsDelivr and model weights from Hugging Face (and related CDNs). Then inference runs locally in your browser using WebAssembly. Until you consent, the app does not connect to those hosts for AI.
privacyDeviceDataLead: Delete everything Arborito stores in this browser (progress, settings, forum drafts, optional consents), or reset only optional consents for external lesson media, in-browser AI, and inline games. Data you already published on a public network tree is not removed from the network—use the forum and construction tools for that.
privacyResetConsentConfirmBody: We will clear saved choices for external lesson media, in-browser AI (Sage), and the inline-game warning. Your learning progress and backups are not deleted.
privacyWipeLocalBody: This removes all Arborito data stored in this browser (including progress, profile, trees list, sync keys, and forum identity on this device), clears the browser cache used for downloaded in-browser AI models when possible, then reloads the app. It does not erase copies on public networks—use remove online identity, delete posts, or contact the tree publisher if needed. This cannot be undone.
privacyWipeLocalButton: Delete all data on this device
privacyWipeLocalPromptBody: To confirm, type deletetree. This cannot be undone.
privacyTechHosting: Hosting: Static web app (no server-side account for your lessons).
privacyTechCurriculum: Curriculum data: Loaded only from sources you choose (HTTPS, local files, or public nostr:// trees).
privacyTechFonts: Fonts: System fonts only (no Google Fonts or font CDNs).
privacyTechGraph: Styling / graph: Bundled CSS and custom canvas/SVG (no style CDNs).
privacyTechAnalytics: Analytics / ads: None in the app shell (no pixels, tag managers, or social embeds).
privacyNostrRelaysBody:
What they are. Public curricula linked with nostr:// are read and published over the Nostr network. Your browser opens secure WebSockets (wss://) to relays that act as meeting points for tree data and, where applicable, the forum.

Operators and privacy. Each relay is run by an independent third party. They may have their own legal terms and privacy notices; you should read the information published by the operator of the service you are using.

Which relays are used. The exact list depends on the Arborito installation or version you use (for example a website, an internal package, or a copy on your device). If you do not manage that installation yourself, ask whoever provides you access if you need details about which servers are involved.

Already published content. Changing only how your browser connects does not by itself erase information that was already spread on the network; availability or speed may vary depending on the network and operators.

International transfers (GDPR). Relays may be located in different countries. Some versions steer the default configuration towards operators commonly used in the European Union as a practical reference; that does not replace full legal advice when an organisation processes personal data.
privacyText:
1. Data controller

{impressum}

2. Local storage (default)

Local-first: Arborito runs as a client-side application. By default, your progress, settings, and related data are stored locally in your browser (LocalStorage). We do not operate our own central database for your personal learning data.

3. GDPR & who is responsible

If you are in the EU/EEA or UK, the GDPR applies to personal data. For the Arborito software as published by Treesys, use the contact details in the Legal tab. For content and activity in a public online tree (a nostr:// link) or a tree loaded from elsewhere, the person or organisation that publishes or moderates that tree decides what is shared and may be the main contact for questions about that content.

Treesys provides open-source software; it does not review every community tree. Use the in-app controls to delete your posts, remove your online identity for a tree, export a backup file, delete all data on this device (Profile), or clear site data in your browser.

4. Public online trees, forum & sync

When you open a public tree, your browser may connect to peer-to-peer relays. Forum messages are cryptographically signed and readable by others with the same link. Optional learning progress sync is encrypted with keys stored on your device; the network stores ciphertext, not your plaintext progress.

Deletion and moderation use signed records so the app stops showing removed content in the canonical view. Copies may still exist on other devices or caches—this is a general limit of distributed systems.

5. Third-party services

Depending on how you use the app, network requests may reach:

  • Static hosting: Your browser loads the app HTML, scripts, and styles from whatever origin you use (for example a local server or your own deployment).
  • Optional HTTPS trees: If you add an HTTPS URL as a knowledge source, your browser fetches that JSON directly. Arborito does not add analytics or ad scripts.
  • Nostr relays (nostr:// trees): Your browser opens secure WebSockets (wss://) to relays according to the Arborito installation or version you use. Operators may process typical TLS connection metadata and publish their own legal and privacy information. Forum traffic and public curriculum data follow the Nostr protocol.
  • jsDelivr & Hugging Face (optional): Only if you enable In-Browser AI and accept the Sage download prompt: the Transformers.js runtime is fetched from jsDelivr and model files from Hugging Face (and related CDNs). The app does not preconnect or prefetch those hosts before you consent.

No marketing stack: No Google Fonts, advertising pixels, tag managers, or social embeds ship with the app shell.

6. Your rights (GDPR)

Depending on context, you may have rights under Articles 15–22 GDPR (access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, objection). You can delete local data with Profile → Delete all data on this device (removes Arborito storage in this browser and reloads the app), or by clearing site data for this origin in your browser settings. You can reset optional consents for external media, in-browser AI, and inline games from Profile without deleting your progress. For data you published to a public tree, use the built-in tools (delete message, remove online identity) and contact the tree publisher if you need further help.
arcadeDesc: Context-aware educational games. Play games that adapt to what you are learning.
gameRecommendedHint: Recommended interactive practice for this topic.
gameRecommendedHintFallback: Recommended interactive practice for this topic.
inlineGameWarning: You are about to load an external game that may include third-party content. Continue? (This notice will not be shown again.)
arcadeDisclaimer: Note: This game can use optional AI to create content as you play.
gameDisclaimer: Arborito acts purely as a visualization client. We assume no responsibility for content generated by third-party AI providers.
plantTreeOpenFailed: Could not open your new tree.
plantTreeOpenFailedReason: Could not open your new tree: {reason}
plantTreeDesc: Create a new local knowledge tree.
treeNameRequired: Enter a name for your tree.
deleteTreeConfirm: Delete this tree and all its content?
editorMagicDraftPrompt: What do you want to change or add in this lesson?
roadmapPhase1Desc: Foundation & Content Growth. Prioritizing quantity and stability.
roadmapPhase2Desc: Community & Collaboration. Teacher integration and AI tools.
roadmapPhase3Desc: Decentralized Ecosystem. Towards accreditation and physical spaces.
graphInsertParentFolderTooltip: Insert a new folder in the tree that wraps this item (same place under its current parent).
graphPromptNewParentFolder: Name for the new parent folder:
graphInsertParentDenied: The curriculum root cannot be wrapped in a new folder.
graphInsertParentSuccess: Folder inserted in the tree above this item.
graphInsertParentFailed: Could not insert folder in the tree.
graphCreateParentError: Could not resolve the parent folder for the new item.
movePickOnTreeHint: Navigate to the destination folder, then tap Move here.
movePickOnTreeBanner: Navigate to the destination folder, then tap Move here.
moveNoTargets: No valid folder to move into.
moveLocalUnsupported: In a local garden you can move topics: use Move on the node, then pick the destination folder (on mobile in construction, open the folder and tap Move here).
graphMoveLocalUseTree: Reorganize topics with Move in the course tree; path-based moves are not used for local gardens.
graphMoveDisabledRoot: The course start cannot be moved.
graphFolderToolsScopeHint: These actions apply to the folder shown above: new items are created inside it. The highlighted row below is a topic or lesson inside that folder.
manualIntroText: Think of your course as a tree: big topics branch into smaller ones, and each lesson is like a leaf you open when you are ready. You choose where to go next—there is no forced order unless you want one.
manualPhilosophyDesc: Courses can come from a link someone shared, a file, or your own edits. Arborito keeps what you are studying on this device until you export a backup or sign in online—no central “cloud classroom” you have to trust by default.
manualNavDesc: Move around the map the way you would a picture: drag empty space to slide, zoom in when text is small, and tap circles to open the next level.
manualNavPanDesc: Drag the sky or empty background (not the nodes) to slide the map.
manualNavZoomDesc: Use the scroll wheel or the +/− buttons.
manualNavExpandDesc: Tap or click a circle to reveal what sits underneath.
manualNavFocusDesc: Double-click a node to steady the camera on that topic.
manualLearnDesc: Roughly, the levels read from big to small:
manualGardenDesc: Profile turns progress into simple metaphors so you can see momentum at a glance.
manualGardenSeeds: Seeds — earned when you finish a module; they fill your garden view.
manualGardenStreak: Streak — consecutive days you opened Arborito; a gentle nudge, not a grade.
manualGardenXP: Photosynthesis (XP) — points from reading; light gamification, easy to ignore if you prefer.
manualGardenMemory: Memory — older topics can gently fade to remind you to revisit; Arcade and quick rereads help.
manualArcadeDesc: Short games can use the lesson you have open so practice stays close to what you are studying.
manualConstructDesc: When you are allowed to edit a course, construction mode adds clear buttons to add, edit, or move items while you stay on the map.
manualDataDesc: By default your progress stays in this browser. Use Profile → export for a file you can keep safe; import brings it back here or on another computer.
manualDataPresence: For public trees on the network, Arborito shows a rough seed signal (recent browsers with the tree open). It is not a perfect census, but it reads like a pulse: more simultaneous readers usually makes the tree feel more alive for learners and maintainers. On a local tree this does not apply—everything stays on your device until you publish or share a copy.
manualLearnRootsDesc: Broad subjects at the top of the tree.
manualLearnModulesDesc: Folders that group related lessons together.
manualLearnLessonsDesc: Reading pages—open one when you are ready to study that topic.
manualLearnExamsDesc: Optional challenges spanning a branch; doing well marks stronger mastery on that path.
manualArcadeContextBody: Games can read the lesson you have open so drills match your topic. If you turn on the Owl (AI), it may suggest challenges from that text—you can ignore it completely.
manualSageIntro: The Owl (Sage) is an optional helper for explanations or short summaries. You stay in control of when it runs.
manualSageInBrowser: Small AI models can run entirely on your device when you enable them—nothing is sent to Arborito for that.
manualSageLocalDesc: You can also point Sage at an assistant running on your own computer for a more private setup.
manualConstructStep1: Turn on construction mode with the hard-hat icon in the toolbar or under “More”.
manualConstructStep2: Tap a topic on the map: you will see options to add a folder or a lesson. On phones, use the bottom bar (Back, Undo, Publish, Owl, More) for extra settings.
manualConstructStep3: Tap the pencil to edit: the lesson opens in the side panel.
manualConstructStep4: To move something, select it and use Move, then pick the destination folder (list in the modal on desktop, or on mobile construction open the folder and tap Move here).
manualConstructStep5: With the tree root selected, use +folder / +lesson in the panel header for top-level items. While you build, the usual dock is replaced by the construction bar.
manualAuthoringBody:
Recommended: write and polish lessons inside Arborito, then publish online from the app or keep a private local copy. Share a link or an export file—no command line needed.

Advanced teams can still pair Arborito with their own websites or repos; that is optional.
manualDataExport: Export — in Profile, download your backpack file and keep a copy somewhere safe (USB, cloud drive you trust, etc.).
manualDataImport: Import — pick that file in Profile on this device or another to bring your progress back.
releasesConfirmDeleteBody: Are you sure you want to remove '{version}'?
sageArchitectIntro: 👷 Ready to build. What shall we plant?
arcadeGardenEmptyDesc: Complete lessons to start cultivating your memory garden.
arcadeWitheredMsg: These lessons are fading from memory. Play to refresh them!
conRevertTooltip: Open Trees to switch course or reload from disk
conGovTooltip: Collaborators, publish online, or invite editors.
defaultLessonContent: The lesson name is the node title (field above). Write the lesson body here: intro, lists, etc. Add outline steps from the lesson menu with “New part” and “New sub-step”, or use the Part / Subpart buttons on the bar.
defaultLessonEditHint: In construction mode, the editor opens automatically when you open this lesson. Replace this text there, or use Edit in the tree toolbar after closing the editor.
backpackDesc: JSON backup of your profile and progress in this browser.
profileBackpackDescLocalOnly: Export a file before you clear the browser or switch devices.
profileBackpackDescCloudOn: Cloud sync is on; the file is an extra copy if you want one.
profileBackpackDescCloudOff: Sync is off — export if you want a file backup.
profileBackpackFileHint: Profile, progress, and saved trees. Signing out deletes this device.
profileCloudSyncAriaLabel: Turn cloud progress sync on or off
profileUpdateDisplayHint: Tap your avatar to change it. Save when you’re ready — the button activates after you edit.
profileSyncRiskZoneLead: Rotating your secret or deleting the online account can lock out other devices or remove network sign-in. Use only if you understand the consequences.
profileModeLocal: Local only — not signed in online
graphPromptFolderNameFriendly: You are creating a module inside “{parent}”. What should it be called?
graphPromptLessonNameFriendly: You are creating a lesson inside “{parent}”. What should it be called?
graphFolderCreatedOk: Module created. Add lessons inside it next.
graphLessonCreatedOk: Lesson created. You can edit its content now.
sageBuildLocalOnly: You can only build in a local garden.
releasesCopyFromPreviousHint: Stores a frozen copy for this tag: uses the latest archived snapshot if one exists; otherwise the tree you have open now (live / rolling).
releasesCreateFormHint: Enter a short tag (e.g. v2) and tap Create. The app saves this tree under that version when it can.
releasesVersionCreatedBody: Version folder "{name}" was created. No course copy was saved (nothing to snapshot yet).
releasesVersionCreatedWithSnapshotBody: Version "{name}" is saved with a copy of your course in this tree.
arcadeDeleteSaveConfirm: Delete save data for this game?
pdfPopupBlocked: Pop-up blocked. Allow pop-ups for PDF generation.
editorMagicDraftExample: e.g. Add worked examples to section 2
gameStorageFullWarn: Storage full. Delete old saves in the Arcade menu.
sageSettingsBrowserDesc: Runs directly in your browser using the CPU. Data stays on this device.
sageSettingsStableHordeDesc: Use the decentralized StableHorde network to process text. Data is sent to the cloud.
sageStableHordePrivacyNote: Using StableHorde means accepting https://stablehorde.net/privacy - Prompts are processed in the cloud.
sageStableHordeDisclaimer: Stable Horde is a community-driven AI service. Arborito is not responsible for its content, availability, or privacy practices.
sageStableHordeModelHint: Click a model above to select it, or leave empty to auto-select the first available.
sageBlueprintStructureMessage: Here is the structure you requested.
editorToolbarMagicAiHint: Magic draft (AI): describe a topic to fill the lesson body
editorTitleAttrH1: Main lesson title in the text (#)
editorTitleAttrH2: Large outline step (##). Same as “New part” in the lesson menu.
editorTitleAttrH3: Small outline step (###). Same as “New sub-step” in the lesson menu.
profileSyncBlurb: Export/import a small JSON for backup or moving devices.
gameConsentAiNotice: This game uses AI. To run locally, Arborito must download model files from the Hugging Face CDN.
gameAiErrorLead: Nothing is wrong on your side. You can try again in a moment.
gameAiErrorOllama: Could not connect to local AI (Ollama). Make sure it is running on localhost:11434.
gameAiErrorBrowser: The learning helper isn’t ready yet. Open the Sage menu and finish initializing in-browser AI.
gameAiErrorInitFailed: The learning helper could not finish starting.
gameCrashedLead: Game player — loading issue. You didn’t do anything wrong.
forumSelfDeleteConfirm: Delete your message? This removes it from the public view of this tree.
sourcesUnifiedAsk: What do you want to learn?
sourcesUnifiedHint: Search and load trees (local, saved, and Internet).
sourcesLocalEmpty: Your garden is empty. Plant your first tree.
sourcesGlobalReportTooSoon: You already reported this tree recently.
curriculumSwitcherTruncBrowseHint: Showing first matches only. Narrow your search.
curriculumSwitcherTruncVersionsHint: Showing first versions only. Narrow your search or open Sources for the full list.
creatorReportsToastOne: Your tree received a recent directory report. Check Trees / reports if relevant.
creatorReportsToastMany: Your tree received {n} recent directory reports. Check Trees / reports if relevant.
sourcesUnifiedInternetSortHint: Discover blends freshness and engagement (likes & usage). With no signals, older listings sink; Recent and Most used are straight sorts.
sourcesGlobalPartialBody: We only load a limited batch per query (the network is huge). Try more specific keywords, use a share code, or check again later.
sourcesGlobalTorrentIndexLagHint: Optional torrent mirror: the public list updates on a schedule, so very new trees may appear there after a short delay. Live network metadata is usually ahead.
sourcesGlobalSearchHint: Search title, description, or public author name. Results are always partial—refine your query or use a code.
sourcesUnifiedListTruncBody: This combined view shows at most {{n}} rows after sorting. Refine the search or use Filters to show only one source type.
sourcesGlobalEditOwnDenied: Only this device with the publisher key can edit this tree.
sourcesGlobalVoteTooSoon: Please wait a few minutes before voting.
sourcesCtaTitle: Looking for a tree, or creating one?
sourcesCtaBody: This screen is for searching and loading trees. If you want to create a new one (author mode), tap “Create tree”.
onboardingBody: A free learning app built around knowledge maps (trees). Pick a language below, then continue to choose or add a tree.
onboardingBetaWarning: Heavy development. Nothing is guaranteed—stability, your data, or outcomes.
onboardingPrivacyText: This application uses the Nostr protocol to ensure you own your content. To function, the app connects to German and European servers (relay.tchncs.de, haven.relayted.de, and snort.social). By continuing, you accept that your IP address (technical data required for the connection) and your public key will be processed by these nodes under EU regulations.
arboritoDonateNotAccepting: Arborito does not accept donations yet; this button is informational only.
sourcesGlobalDelistConfirm: This publishes a signed “delist” signal for your own tree so directory clients can hide it. This does not delete content from the network.
maintainerBlocklistLoadRefused: This public tree is on the maintainer blocklist for this app build: it cannot be opened from a link or share code here. Automatic moderation (reports / legal dispute) does not block opening—only this curated list does. To appeal, use the contact or policy channel described for your distribution (e.g. README / email).
maintainerBlocklistAddRefused: This tree cannot be added: it is on the maintainer blocklist for this build.
sourcesGlobalHiddenHint: Community signals hide this listing from the public directory; only you (the owner) see it here. Anyone with a direct link may still open the tree in the app.
legalReportWhereInTreeBody: Where in this tree is the infringing material? Be specific: paths, lesson or deck titles, on-screen text, or node ids. Vague one-line reports are not useful.
legalReportWhereInTreePlaceholder: e.g. Module "Basics" → lesson "…" — the first card shows …
legalReportWhereTooShort: Please describe the location inside this tree more precisely (at least {n} characters).
legalReportWhatWorkBody: Which work is being used without permission? Give title, author or rights holder, and a public link if one exists (store page, ISBN page, etc.).
legalReportWhatWorkPlaceholder: Title, creator, link to the original listing…
legalReportWhatWorkTooShort: Please identify the work more clearly (at least {n} characters).
legalReportDescBody: Explain how this tree copies or closely imitates that work (min 200 characters).
legalReportLinksBody: Optional evidence links (one per line): official page, catalog, screenshot host, etc.
legalReportDeclarationBody: I declare in good faith that the tree locations and work identification above are accurate to the best of my knowledge.
legalReportSent: Signed legal report stored on the network. This app does not relay messages between claimant and owner. Proof of settlement or withdrawal is outside the app (lawyer, court, or—if we add it later—a signed “withdraw” on the network). The distributor is not auto-notified and does not certify outcomes: use their contact only for app abuse or blocklist policy, not to mediate copyright disputes.
creatorLegalDisputeToast: Legal notice on your tree: the public directory no longer shows this listing to others until you respond. From Trees, open your row ⋯ → owner response (signed). You have about 48 hours to act; after that, follow-up is on you procedurally—this app only records your signed acknowledgment on the network.
sourcesLegalDisputeOwnerBefore48h: Legal claim open: the public directory does not show this listing to others until it is resolved. You have about {hours} hour(s) to submit your signed owner response under ⋯ (recommended within 48 hours).
sourcesLegalDisputeOwnerAfter48h: More than 48 hours have passed since the latest legal notice without your signed owner response. The listing remains hidden from the public directory; submit your owner response below to record your position on-chain.
sourcesGlobalLegalDefenseNotOwner: Only the tree owner on this device can sign this response.
sourcesGlobalLegalDefenseNoReport: Could not load the latest legal timestamp. Try again shortly.
sourcesGlobalLegalDefenseFailed: Could not publish the owner response.
legalOwnerDefenseConfirmBody: Your signed response ties to the timestamp of the latest legal report. Where the law requires it, minimal metadata may be shared with competent authorities. Continue?
